steps.label: cause
steps.text: A producer inoculates the must with a selected cultured yeast strain in a sufficient viable population.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The chosen strain establishes quickly and has known fermentation tolerances and performance characteristics.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Fermentation is generally faster to establish and more predictable than relying only on an uncontrolled indigenous population.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Selected yeast increases fermentation predictability through rapid establishment and known performance.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
